card.title: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Unify phone, browser, and installed-app geometry so menu, play, overlays, touch controls, status, and maze framing share measured safe-content rules.
card.objective: Ship and prove the physical-iPhone controls, timer, maze-fit, and spacing correction on canonical production, then close only after exact device acceptance.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu, Options, play, and Pause remain contained without overlap, clipping, or wasteful dead space.
One finger slides continuously across the D-pad and the stick remains smooth, lower, wide-travel, and directionally stable.
The phone maze border remains edge-tight while every maze size scales its inner tiles to fill the stable frame.
Player and AI timers stop on goal arrival and remain frozen through lifecycle transitions.
Fresh physical-iPhone production review accepts the exact deployed build.
discoveries: PR #76 merged cleanly and the exact merge was deployed to canonical production.
Public production passed the full phone surface matrix plus arrow and stick goal-route lifecycle proof.
nextActions: Review canonical production on physical iPhone Safari and installed PWA where available.
Confirm controls, timer, maze framing, spacing, and motion feel; return screenshots or explicit clean-device acceptance.
Move the exact card to Completed only after that acceptance.
blockers: Fresh operator physical-iPhone acceptance of this exact production deployment remains required.
evidence: Vercel production: dpl_3nC5JWfZMG86Vo9JbjKus32cyDtF, READY, https://fawxzzy-mazer.vercel.app, main-nOKYHIwr.js.
Production arrow and stick routes: 99/99 and 102/102 moves, goals reached, frozen timers, lifecycle and fresh-world gates passed.

entry.headline: Physical controls and phone layout correction is live and production-proven
completed: Merged PR #76 at exact merge commit 55c26744b6bb59f3ccf9b5210e3475f7b09a7ca7.
Deployed that exact merge to canonical Mazer production as dpl_3nC5JWfZMG86Vo9JbjKus32cyDtF.
Passed the public 39/39 mobile surface matrix and both production control-mode goal routes.
discovered: The production service-worker update reload must be isolated from Playwright proof; blocking it only in the disposable QA context produced stable exact runtime evidence without changing production.
next: Collect the operator's fresh physical-iPhone acceptance and then complete the card through guarded transfer/readback.
blockers: Physical-device acceptance is external and not yet supplied for this deployment.

card.title: normalize browser UI layout across viewports
card.summary: Normalize responsive geometry so maze, menu, overlay, and control layouts derive from the active content rectangle without persisting stale viewport dimensions.
card.objective: Keep one tight phone maze frame across maze sizes while scaling its inner tiles from current viewport and maze data.
acceptanceCriteria: No viewport-derived geometry is persisted across incompatible browser states.
The phone maze border keeps a stable edge-tight margin across supported maze dimensions.
Inner maze tiles scale to fill the stable border without clipping or escaping it.
Mobile, native maximize, restore, and browser-chrome transitions have deterministic proof.
discoveries: The stable phone frame and derived inner tile scale are live in production.
The complete production phone surface family passed without layout or runtime failures.
nextActions: Verify multiple production maze sizes on physical iPhone keep the same edge-tight frame.
Complete native maximize, restore, and browser-chrome transition automation.
Complete only after both proof gates pass.
blockers: Physical-device multi-size acceptance and native window/browser-chrome automation remain open.

entry.headline: Maze-size-independent phone framing is live in production
completed: Released the stable 8px phone outer-frame and maze-size-derived inner tile scale.
Verified the full public production surface family at 390x844 DPR-3.
discovered: Production browser proof is clean; physical multi-size and native desktop-window proof remain separate acceptance gates.
next: Collect physical-iPhone multi-size proof and complete native maximize/restore/browser-chrome automation.

card.title: shared menu and play run status panel
card.summary: Use one two-row status component in menu AI and active play for time, contextual rank, score out of 100, and maze level.
card.objective: Keep shared status geometry contained and freeze player and AI elapsed time at exact goal arrival through all lifecycle transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu and play consume the same contained status layout.
Player and AI timers freeze immediately on the end tile.
The frozen value remains unchanged through goal hold, deconstruction, handoff, and rebuild.
Completion telemetry consumes the same duration.
Displayed score remains traceable to the canonical run-quality contract.
discoveries: The timer correction is live in canonical production.
Both production control modes reached the goal, preserved lifecycle locks, and retained exact frozen values on resample.
nextActions: Verify player and AI timer freeze on physical iPhone production.
Confirm final score-field authority before terminal completion.
Move to Completed only after both gates pass.
blockers: Physical-device timing acceptance and canonical score-field traceability remain open.
evidence: Production arrow route: 99/99, goal reached, timer frozen at 9,585ms.
Production stick route: 102/102, goal reached, timer frozen at 26,190ms.
Both routes passed goal-hold, deconstruction, handoff, building, fresh-ready, input-lock, and world-turn gates.

entry.headline: Immediate player and AI goal-timer freeze is live and production-proven
completed: Released the shared frozen elapsed-time lifecycle to canonical production.
Proved both production touch modes reach the goal with exact stable timer resamples and full lifecycle handoff.
discovered: Automated production behavior is clean; physical-device observation and score authority remain explicit terminal gates.
next: Collect physical-iPhone timer acceptance and close canonical score traceability.
